# bunWay

> Express-compatible web framework for Bun. Drop-in replacement with zero rewrites.

## What is bunWay?

bunWay is a web framework for Bun that uses the same API as Express.js. If you know Express, you know bunWay. No learning curve, just faster performance.

## Key Features

- Express-compatible API: Same `app.get()`, `app.post()`, `app.route()`, `req`, `res`, `next` patterns
- Chainable routes: `app.route('/path').get(...).post(...).put(...)` for cleaner route definitions
- mergeParams: `new Router({ mergeParams: true })` to access parent route parameters in child routers
- Bun-native: Built specifically for Bun runtime, no Node.js polyfills
- Zero dependencies: No production dependencies
- Built-in middleware: Body parsing (JSON, urlencoded, text, raw), file uploads (multer-compatible), CORS, cookies, sessions, static files, rate limiting
- TypeScript-first: Full type safety out of the box
- Graceful shutdown: app.close(callback?) with callback and Promise support
- HTTPS/TLS: app.listen({ tls: { cert, key } }) for native TLS
- Server access: app.server getter for Bun.Server instance
- X-Forwarded-Proto: req.protocol respects proxy headers when trust proxy is enabled
- req.fresh / req.stale: Cache validation (ETag + Last-Modified). Only GET/HEAD with 2xx/304.
- req.range(size, options?): Parses Range header. Returns array of {start,end}, -1 (unsatisfiable), -2 (malformed), or undefined.
- res.sendFile() range support: Automatic 206 Partial Content with Content-Range header. Accept-Ranges: bytes on all responses.
- res.jsonp(data): JSONP response. Configurable via app.set("jsonp callback name", "cb"). Sanitizes callback name for XSS prevention.
- req.res / res.req / res.app: Cross-references between request, response, and app objects.
- app.use([path1, path2], handler): Array path support for middleware and sub-router mounting.

## Installation

```bash
bun add bunway
```

## Quick Start

```typescript
import { bunway, json, upload } from 'bunway'

const app = bunway()

app.use(json())

app.get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.json({ message: 'Hello bunWay!' })
})

// File uploads (multer-compatible)
app.post('/upload', upload.single('file'), (req, res) => {
  res.json({ name: req.file.originalname, size: req.file.size })
})

app.listen(3000)
```

- Express Migration: https://bunway.jointops.dev/guide/express-migration
